title: calophyllum gracillimum
intro: Calophyllum gracillimum, also known as the Burmese or Cambodian canarium, is a species of tree native to Southeast Asia. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties, making it useful for treating various health conditions. The health benefits of Calophyllum gracillimum include relief from skin irritations, wounds, and fungal infections, as well as its potential to lower blood pressure and cholesterol levels. In traditional medicine, the plant is often prepared as a herbal tea, ointment, or oil, which is applied topically or consumed orally to harness its medicinal properties.
